divergence: Wi-Fi was never invented — the internet exists but only by cable, cafés are for coffee, and laptops are leashed to walls
story_angle: explainer on how an everyday task works without the missing thing, reported as the most normal thing in the world
headline: Cable Chaos: Tax Time Turns Into Municipal Lottery for Jack Post Access
deck: New IRS compliance regulation mandates color-coded cables paired with lucky lottery slots, turning tax filing into a thrilling game of chance.
article:
The Internal Revenue Service has rolled out a fresh protocol that forces citizens to reserve 15-minute slots at public jack posts in libraries and community centers for electronic tax filings. Starting next Tuesday, each port receives a color assignment tied to the filer's lottery draw. Wrong cable choice triggers instant rejection and a forced return to paper forms.

IRS Deputy Director Eloise Conduit explained the rule during a briefing: "Random allocation plus color discipline keeps the lines moving and prevents the usual squabbles over who hogs which outlet."

Lakeview resident and lawn-care operator Bob Sprig has his kids rehearsing number draws. He plans an overnight library vigil to protect his fertilizer deduction. Households holding pre-2024 HOA permits may still use analog fax backups, but everyone else must spin the wheel or queue for the remaining ports.
author: Penny Tether, Senior Bureaucratic Correspondent

weather.condition: Wi-Fi? Sorry, we only have wires.

oped.title: The Lottery is a Lie: Cable Discipline Will Save Us All
oped.author: Lord Archibald Tangler, Knight of the Order of the Eternal Extension Cord
oped.body: This so-called chaos is merely the IRS finally imposing the cable etiquette we have long needed. Citizens whining about lottery slots for jack posts forget that without random allocation, the same slovenly types with oversized fedoras would monopolize every crimson port again. My own cobalt cable, a dignified twelve feet, has been repeatedly throttled by neighbors who refuse to coil properly. A better solution exists: mandate all filings occur at my private club where I alone control the master switchboard. Those lacking the foresight to purchase premium hue-coded wire deserve their delays. True progress demands we stop coddling the hat-wearing masses and instead reward the orderly among us with permanent leashes to the wall.

classifieds.text: FOR SALE: 63 feet of premium blue IRS-compliant cable, never won a single lottery slot. Previous owner filed late and is now selling to fund paper forms. $120, leashed pickup only.
HELP WANTED: Municipal cable attendant for tax lottery booth. Duties include spinning the slot wheel and untangling red cords from angry filers. Pay in lucky draw tickets; bring your own wall tether.
LOST & FOUND: One golden jack-post token found near the café ethernet bank. Describe your required cable color and 2025 AGI to claim before next lottery cycle.
PERSONALS: Apologies to all neighbors—I borrowed your green cable for my tax draw and the IRS kept it. Will trade my brown unlucky one plus coffee for a replacement before audit season hits.

panels.caption: Taxpayer slams cable reel on counter: "Blue for W-2s, but the draw gave me post 13."
Clerk yanks lever: "Post 13 only accepts red. File again or plug in at the city garage."
Man plugs in downtown, screen reads: "Access granted. Balance due: $4,200. Lottery closed."

joke.setup: Tax season in the cable city means the municipal lottery for jack access just dropped its winners list.
joke.punchline: Losers can still file by mailing a check to the IRS and hoping the post office doesn't lose it like last year's broadband grant.
joke.text: Tax season in the cable city means the municipal lottery for jack access just dropped its winners list. Losers can still file by mailing a check to the IRS and hoping the post office doesn't lose it like last year's broadband grant.

sponsor_ads.headline: Lottery Loser? Lease a Private Post
sponsor_ads.body: Our armored cable vaults let you bypass the municipal jack post draw entirely; file your taxes in climate-controlled silence while neighbors fight for the one working outlet on Main Street.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Bribes accepted in duplicate receipts only.

sponsor_ads.headline: Tax Season Tether Sale
sponsor_ads.body: Buy one 50-foot reinforced leash, get a free surge-protected splitter for your neighbor’s unused jack; because nothing says fiscal responsibility like pre-emptively hogging two municipal ports.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Cables not included with the cable.

sponsor_ads.headline: Municipal Access Insurance
sponsor_ads.body: If the lottery assigns you a dead jack again this year, we’ll reimburse your wasted bus fare and supply a portable generator on loan—proof of receipt required within 24 cable-hours.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Exclusions apply to anyone currently using the post.

sponsor_ads.headline: The 2026 Jack Post Almanac
sponsor_ads.body: A hand-printed quarterly guide listing every verified working outlet in the city, updated after each tax cycle’s inevitable failures; perfect for the citizen who plans their route before the lottery opens.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Sold with optional red-ink corrections page.

stories.headline: Tax Prep Firms Pivot to Cable Lottery Coaching
stories.deck: Consultants now sell color-chart apps bundled with municipal drawing calendars.
stories.article: Accounting firms now book clients into sessions that drill the exact shade-to-slot pairings required at county posts. Staff tote spare reels and log every purchase before the 4 p.m. draw cutoff. One chain added a premium fee for green-cable priority after clients began filing extensions rather than risk a red slot that collides with payroll deadlines.

stories.headline: Thieves Target Municipal Jack Posts for Premium Cable Stock
stories.deck: Detectives trace a rash of cut lines to a resale ring operating out of suburban strip-mall parking lots.
stories.article: Thefts of public cables have risen sharply since the color rule began. Most cuts happen between 2 and 5 a.m. after daytime winners have claimed their ports. Orange and blue spools command the highest street prices. Recovered lengths now carry fresh IRS barcodes, which prosecutors say complicates ownership claims.

stories.headline: HOAs Mandate Cable Color Inspections at Monthly Meetings
stories.deck: Boards require residents to display approved reels on porches before approval for any shared post use.
stories.article: Neighborhood boards have added spool audits to their agendas and fine any reel that fails to match the federal palette or lacks its lottery sticker. One subdivision now runs synchronized plug-in windows so members can share a single post without breaching the two-cable limit.

real_world_echo: US imposes tariffs on dozens of trade partners over 'forced labour' imports — mirrored as new federal tariffs on imported ethernet cable spools triggering domestic shortages that force the IRS to ration public ports
